text: 英語聽力模擬視題,17.

來回答有關小題和閱讀像小題,

It's said that this year's Odd Soxtape falls on November 13th.
Odd Soxtape?
What's it about, Amy?
Basically, participants wear mismatched socks to schools, workplaces, or at home,
to stand against prejudice and accept differences.
I participated in Odd Soxtape 2024, which was on November 12th.
That sounds meaningful, so you wore mismatched socks to school that day?
Yeah, I wore a blue knee-high sock on my left foot and a yellow ankle sock on my right.
Sounds cool. Besides wearing odd socks, what else can participants do?
They can post photos of their mismatched socks on social media or donate money to the official website of this event.
I donated all my pocket money, five pounds to support the cause.
Can I celebrate Odd Soxtape with you on November 13th?
Sure, but this date is not the one officially announced on the official website.
I'll let you know when the exact date is announced.

Can you recommend some TV dramas that are suitable for teenagers?
Sure, first, there's the Baby Sitters Club. The show is based on the popular series of books by N.M. Martin.
It's about a group of friends who start a babysitting business.
However, each girl faces her own personal challenges.
I'm not really into that kind of theme. Is there anything with a more unique plot?
Absolutely. Try Hannah Montana. The main character, Miley Stewart, needs a double life.
To most people, she's just a couple of people.

To most people, she's just a normal teenager, but her closest family and friends know her secret.
She's also a famous pop singer.
Sounds interesting. I'll watch it.
I also recommend another show called Keenan and Kel. It first aired in August 1996 and concluded in May 2000.
Oh, I've seen this drama before, but I didn't finish it because it felt a bit outdated.

In 2018, Miley's husband Marlon died in a skiing accident. His sudden death turned Miley's world upside down.
When Carla, one of Miley's closest friends, heard the awful news, she knew right away that she wanted to help.
Carla and Miley met 10 years ago at a parent-teacher conference. Carla's son and Miley's daughter were classmates.
They had a lot in common. They both volunteered in the community and loved cooking and hiking.
Carla knew Miley already had crucial daily support from her sister, Kathy O'Brien, who had come to stay with her.
But she wanted to do more for Miley.
She created a rolling calendar where 31 women, the calendar girls, would each do a small thing for Miley every month on a specific day.
Carla made sure to note which dates might be especially hard. Miley's birthday in April, the couple's anniversary in July, and Marlon's birthday in August.
Throughout the year, Miley's calendar girls supported her in various ways.
Nicole, who lived three houses away, brought delicious hot meals, along with flowers and a card.
Tony, who also lived in the neighborhood, would often meet Miley for a walk.
When Miley looks back on the year after Marlon died, it is with both deep sadness and deep gratitude.
I hadn't had any really huge losses before, so I don't think I knew the value of having that much support, she reflects.
